rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YURIKO OSHITA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/17/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (2) The unverified supplement to the Petition for Probate, which supplement was filed on 6/22/22, did not clearly indicate whose child each grandchild is -- presumably all the children of the executor -- so it is not entirely clear who would take as a result of the disclaimer by Eiko Judith Masai. Does Eiko not have any children or more remote issue? (3) The I&A was not signed and verified; the executor signed only the Statement About the Bond. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 10/6/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: Detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sale must be filed to support the statutory fee. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).])

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DONALD L. YOST TRUST UNDER AGREEMENT DTD 3/16/12
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining That Certain Assets Are Included In Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/17/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The petitioner, successor co-trustee, lives in Contra Costa County, where he presumably administers the trust, unless his sister (and other successor co-trustee) in Massachusetts administers the trust there. In any event, the trust is not administered in San Francisco. (2) Instructions for how to appear remotely are set forth on the court's website under Calendar Information: https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. The instructions must be printed out and attached to the notice of hearing that is mailed to all interested parties. LRSF 14.1 D.6 cites to https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court, not the website Counsel reportedly tried to access. (3) No courtesy copies of papers filed on 6/20/23 and thereafter have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] In hopes of not continuing the hearing again, the papers were skimmed online. (4) The proof of service filed on 9/5/23 shows that an amended petition was filed. No amended petition appears on the register of actions. If the petition is not withdrawn, courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 10/6/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LATONYA GRACE ROGERS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Existence And Internal Affairs Of A Trust And To Determine Whether Successor Trustee John David Barry Shall Be Deemed To Have Predeceased Settlor Latonya Rogers By Clear And Convincing Evidence Of Undue Influence
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues since the initial hearing on 06/13/2023: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) and actually served with a copy of the DE-120 form must be attached to the proof of service. 2) If the petitioner is not a current or former beneficiary or trustee of the case name trust, does the petitioner have standing to seek relief under Probate Code section 17200(a) concerning the existence or internal affairs of that trust? 3) If no conservator has been appointed for the settlor of the case name trust, does the probate department have concurrent jurisdiction over the financial abuse claim under section 15657.3 of the Welfare & Institutions Code? Even if the probate department is to accept the jurisdiction, no summons has been issued and served on John Barry. 4) If the petitioner is requesting an order directing John Barry to transfer the subject real property (or proceeds if sold) to the trustee of Eddie Rogers' trust (or to the personal representative of the estate of Latonya Rogers), should the petitioner be required to amend the petition to seek the relief under Probate Code sections 850 & 856? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JAMES A. MCKENZIE AND MARINA MCKENZIE 1989 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition: (1) To Remove Trustee, Appoint Temporary Trustee/Receiver And Suspend Power Of Trustee Pending Trial; (2) To Interpret Trust Provisions; (3) To Surcharge Trustee; (4) For Damages For Fraud And Breach Of Trustee'S Fiduciary Duties; (5) For An Accounting; (6) For Final Distribution And Termination Of Trust; And (7) For Attorneys' Fee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/24/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Trusts require 30 days' notice. Notice mailed on 9/6/23 for this hearing was insufficient notice. (2) Notice was mailed to Charles McKenzie at an address that differs from the one shown in the petition. (3) Why is William F. McKenzie not listed among the beneficiaries entitled to notice? If he is deceased, when did he die and who is/are his heirs or successor(s)-in-interest (depending on when he died)? (4) Notice must be given directly to a person entitled to notice, not in care of another. [CRC 7.51(a).]. (5) If anyone entitled to notice is a minor, notice must be sent directly to the minor. A separate copy of the notice must be sent to the person or persons having legal custody of the minor, with whom the minor resides. (That person must be clearly identified in the petition as the custodial person.) (6) The petition does not identify whom the Court should appoint as a receiver or temporary [successor] trustee. That person must be identified and must consent. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 10/13/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WYN WANG LEUNG IRREVOCABLE TRUST DATED APRIL 20, TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/31/2023. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No statement of the TD Ameritrade IRA account (2221) covering 03/31/2020 is on file. 2) The total amount of receipts stated on the summary of account (item 3) does not match the total amount of income receipts stated on Schedule A. 3) Schedule A does not show any proceeds of the promissory notes and other loans the trust borrowed from the petitioner. 4) Schedule A does not show the $1,750 security deposit as a receipt item (only listed as a liability). 5) Schedule C shows the mortgage payments entirely allocated to income. Did the mortgage payments reduce no principal balance? 6) Schedules E and F must be revised to remove the promissory notes and other loans as the negative cash balance. 7) The Wells Fargo checking account 8411 is not titled under the name of this trust. Is it a trust asset? 8) The IRA, 401k, and other retirement accounts are not assets of the trust, as title to those assets cannot be transferred to the trust by applicable federal law. If the petitioner intended to request court approval of her acts taken as attorney-in-fact under the settlor's power of attorney re the non-trust retirement accounts, a separate petition must be filed in a new case under the name of the power of attorney. See Probate Code sections 4500 et seq.
